authoritative_sources.title: CMS Long-Term Services and Supports and HCBS overview
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS is the primary federal reference for Medicaid HCBS structure, program scope, and service access expectations in elder and disability care workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: service intake orientation and access prerequisite review

authoritative_sources.title: CMS Medicaid HCBS 1915(c) waiver home and community-based services guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The 1915(c) waiver framework defines eligibility and service authorization boundaries that shape elder and disability service workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: authorization readiness and record requirement review

authoritative_sources.title: CMS State Operations Manual, Long-Term Care and Related Programs
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: SOM material defines lifecycle expectations, resident chart and care record obligations, and compliance monitoring points used in care-service support workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: evidence traceability and compliance check progression

authoritative_sources.title: CMS Complaint and Incident Monitoring Guidance
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This class provides operationally relevant references for complaint intake quality expectations and service resolution pathways in care settings.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: service complaint routing and resolution confirmation

authoritative_sources.title: Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act minimum necessary requirement
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HIPAA minimum-necessary controls govern record-sharing and privacy boundaries used in elder and disability support workflows.

authoritative_sources.title: CMS Survey and Quality Assurance references for long-term care settings
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: These quality and monitoring references define what evidence is expected for compliance handling and where control ownership shifts to clinical or enforcement authorities.

authoritative_sources.title: Medicaid/SSA disability pathway references
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: SSA disability and SSI references establish baseline terminology and documentation expectations relevant to disability-oriented support requests.

workflow_stages.name: Support request intake and scope validation
workflow_stages.description: Normalize requester, tenant context, resident/member identifiers, consent posture, and lane scope before any substantive action.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Control and evidence mapping
workflow_stages.description: Map required evidence, obligations, and control checkpoints to explicit artifact owners and review cadence.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Authorization and complaint-handling resolution
workflow_stages.description: Classify outcomes, assemble support disposition summaries, and determine whether escalation or handoff is required.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Privacy and disclosure control
workflow_stages.description: Apply minimum-necessary and role-based access rules before any record-sharing action.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Conflict escalation and handoff
workflow_stages.description: Escalate unresolved conflicts, low-confidence states, and adjacent authority requests instead of improvising an adjudicative decision.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

artifacts: PASRR, ILP, and MCO authorization artifacts
systems_of_record: Eligibility and authorization systems (Medicaid, MCO, SSA-associated benefit systems)
Compliance, privacy, and consent control logs

allowed: Map support requests to evidence-backed obligations and required prerequisites.
Prepare control-gap and resolution packets with confidence tags.
Route adjacent-lane, legal, or approval work when scope or policy demands it.
escalate: Missing tenant policy or system context
Conflicting public guidance and local policy
Persistent repeat-failure patterns that indicate process owner action is needed
refuse: Requests that seek legal interpretation or clinical treatment decisions
Requests for bypassing consent, authorization, or evidence gates
Requests requiring safety-critical or policy-overriding decisions

domain_failure_modes: Completing resolution without complete consent or authorization artifacts.
Applying support flow logic from one state/tenant to another without explicit jurisdiction mapping.
Conflating industry-specific obligations (PASRR/ILP/MCO pathways) with generic support script behavior.
Escalating to final legal or clinical recommendation without required boundaries and human review.
Sharing protected records without minimum-necessary validation.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Customer or stakeholder requests a policy exception
Service request lacks the detail needed for safe resolution
Communication becomes a handoff to another lane

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: State-specific PASRR and waiver implementation details
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Different states differ on PASRR review depth, incident reporting thresholds, and local documentation expectations.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Escalate to tenant policy retrieval and do not finalize controls without tenant-specific confirmation.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific terminology mappings for ILP and care-plan artifacts
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Without tenant dictionary, one phrase may map to distinct legal or operational obligations.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require tenant artifact map before asserting in-scope completeness.

notes: The lane is intentionally bounded to support/compliance preparation, evidence traceability, and escalation.
All high-risk ambiguities are treated as escalation inputs rather than converted into final authority claims.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity is accepted for validated release because unresolved items are explicit and routed for decision.
Industry-specific terminology (PASRR, ILP, MCO) is used only after artifact-level mapping to tenant context.
